source: CIS AWS COMPUTE SERVICES BENCHMARK V1.1.0
title: Ensure 'assignPublicIp' is set to 'DISABLED' for Amazon ECS services

input_row.source: CIS AWS COMPUTE SERVICES BENCHMARK V1.1.0
input_row.title: Ensure 'assignPublicIp' is set to 'DISABLED' for Amazon ECS services
input_row.description: Ensure that assignPublicIp is set to DISABLED for Amazon ECS services, to restrict direct exposure of containers to the public internet.
input_row.rationale: Enabling public IP assignment could expose container application servers to unintended or unauthorized access. Impact: Disabling assignPublicIp introduces administrative, operational, and potential cost overhead due to the need to configure and maintain private networking and associated resources.
input_row.audit: From Console: 1. Login to the ECS console using https://console.aws.amazon.com/ecs/. 2. In the left panel, click Clusters. 3. Click the name of a cluster. 4. Under Services, click the name of a service. 5. Click Configuration and networking. 6. Under Network configuration, ensure Auto-assign public IP is set to Turned off. 7. Repeat steps 1-6 for each ECS cluster and service. From Command Line: Run the following command to list clusters: aws ecs list-clusters Run the following command to list services in a cluster: aws ecs list-services --cluster <cluster-arn> Run the following command to view the details of a service: aws ecs describe-services --cluster <cluster-arn> --services <service-arn> Under networkConfiguration > awsvpcConfiguration, ensure assignPublicIp is set to DISABLED. Repeat for each cluster and service.
input_row.remediation: From Command Line: For each service requiring remediation, run the following command to set assignPublicIp to DISABLED: aws ecs update-service --cluster <cluster-arn> --service <service-arn> -- network-configuration '{"awsvpcConfiguration":{"subnets":["<subnet- id>"],"securityGroups":["<security-group-id>"],"assignPublicIp":"DISABLED"}}' Default Value: By default, assignPublicIp is set to ENABLED. References: 1. https://awscli.amazonaws.com/v2/documentation/api/latest/reference/ecs/list- clusters.html 2. https://awscli.amazonaws.com/v2/documentation/api/latest/reference/ecs/list- services.html 3. https://awscli.amazonaws.com/v2/documentation/api/latest/reference/ecs/describ e-services.html 4. https://awscli.amazonaws.com/v2/documentation/api/latest/reference/ecs/update- service.html 5. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonECS/latest/APIReference/API_AwsVpcCo nfiguration.html
input_row.impact: Disabling assignPublicIp introduces administrative, operational, and potential cost overhead due to the need to configure and maintain private networking and associated resources.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
- This control involves checking a specific configuration parameter (`assignPublicIp`) in Amazon ECS services, which can be directly queried using AWS APIs.
- The `audit` field provides specific AWS CLI commands that can retrieve this information, indicating that the property in question can be verified programmatically.
- The relevant AWS service, ECS, is well-supported by both the AWS CLI and boto3 SDK, confirming the capability to automate this check.
- Boto3 and AWS CLI have methods to list ECS clusters and services and to describe service configurations, allowing us to examine the `assignPublicIp` setting.
- Therefore, checking the value of `assignPublicIp` is suitable for an automated audit approach.

**PROGRAM NAME:** aws_ecs_service_assign_public_ip_disabled

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- Use the AWS CLI command `aws ecs list-clusters` to enumerate all ECS clusters within the AWS account.
- For each cluster, use `aws ecs list-services --cluster <cluster-arn>` to list all services associated with the cluster.
- For each service, use `aws ecs describe-services --cluster <cluster-arn> --services <service-arn>` to retrieve detailed configuration information.
- Specifically verify that `networkConfiguration.awsvpcConfiguration.assignPublicIp` is set to `DISABLED`.
- Pass criteria: The `assignPublicIp` attribute must be set to `DISABLED` for compliance.
- Fail criteria: If `assignPublicIp` is set to `ENABLED`, the service does not comply with the control.
- This audit process can be implemented using either the AWS CLI or boto3 in a script or CSPM tool to run checks across all regions and accounts to ensure comprehensive coverage.
